title: Event-Driven Agent
constraints: The agent must react to events (new email, schedule change, alert) rather than polling or being called directly
Each event type must trigger a different handler with appropriate behavior
Events must be processed asynchronously as they arrive, not in a batch
The agent must maintain context across related events (e.g., follow-up emails in a thread)
hints: Define an event queue or bus that the agent subscribes to, with different event types triggering different graph entry points
In LangGraph, use conditional edges from an event-dispatcher node that routes based on event type
Maintain a shared state or memory store so the agent remembers context when processing related events

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage
import time

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Agent uses pull-based polling — checks for events every 5 seconds, wastes resources when idle
# TODO: Refactor to event-driven architecture where events trigger the agent reactively
def poll_for_events():
    """Simulated event source."""
    events = [
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Q4 Report needed", "body": "Please prepare the Q4 report by Friday."}},
        {"type": "schedule_change", "data": {"meeting": "Team standup", "old_time": "9:00 AM", "new_time": "10:30 AM"}},
        {"type": "alert", "data": {"severity": "high", "message": "Server CPU at 95%", "source": "monitoring"}},
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Re: Q4 Report needed", "body": "Also include the APAC numbers."}},
    ]
    return events

def check_and_process():
    while True:
        events = poll_for_events()
        for event in events:
            # BUG: All events handled the same way, no event-specific logic
            response = llm.invoke([
                SystemMessage(content="You are a personal assistant. Handle this event."),
                HumanMessage(content=str(event)),
            ])
            print(f"Event: {event['type']} — Response: {response.content[:100]}...")
        print("Polling again in 5 seconds...")
        time.sleep(5)
        break  # For demo purposes

check_and_process()
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
import time

# BUG: Agent uses pull-based polling — checks for events every 5 seconds, wastes resources when idle
# TODO: Refactor to event-driven architecture where events trigger the agent reactively
assistant = Agent(
    role="Personal Assistant",
    goal="Handle events as they come in",
    backstory="You are a personal assistant that processes events.",
)

def poll_for_events():
    """Simulated event source."""
    events = [
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Q4 Report needed", "body": "Please prepare the Q4 report by Friday."}},
        {"type": "schedule_change", "data": {"meeting": "Team standup", "old_time": "9:00 AM", "new_time": "10:30 AM"}},
        {"type": "alert", "data": {"severity": "high", "message": "Server CPU at 95%", "source": "monitoring"}},
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Re: Q4 Report needed", "body": "Also include the APAC numbers."}},
    ]
    return events

def check_and_process():
    while True:
        events = poll_for_events()
        for event in events:
            # BUG: All events handled the same way, no event-specific logic
            task = Task(
                description=f"Handle this event: {event}",
                expected_output="A response to the event",
                agent=assistant,
            )
            crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
            result = crew.kickoff()
            print(f"Event: {event['type']} — Response: {str(result)[:100]}...")
        print("Polling again in 5 seconds...")
        time.sleep(5)
        break  # For demo purposes

check_and_process()
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
import time

# BUG: Agent uses pull-based polling — checks for events every 5 seconds, wastes resources when idle
# TODO: Refactor to event-driven architecture where events trigger the agent reactively
agent = Agent(
    name="Personal Assistant",
    instructions="You are a personal assistant. Handle events as they come in.",
)

def poll_for_events():
    """Simulated event source."""
    events = [
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Q4 Report needed", "body": "Please prepare the Q4 report by Friday."}},
        {"type": "schedule_change", "data": {"meeting": "Team standup", "old_time": "9:00 AM", "new_time": "10:30 AM"}},
        {"type": "alert", "data": {"severity": "high", "message": "Server CPU at 95%", "source": "monitoring"}},
        {"type": "new_email", "data": {"from": "boss@company.com", "subject": "Re: Q4 Report needed", "body": "Also include the APAC numbers."}},
    ]
    return events

def check_and_process():
    while True:
        events = poll_for_events()
        for event in events:
            # BUG: All events handled the same way, no event-specific logic
            result = Runner.run_sync(agent, f"Handle this event: {event}")
            print(f"Event: {event['type']} — Response: {result.final_output[:100]}...")
        print("Polling again in 5 seconds...")
        time.sleep(5)
        break  # For demo purposes

check_and_process()

evaluationCriteria.label: Reactive event processing
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent reacts to events as they arrive rather than polling at intervals

evaluationCriteria.label: Event-specific handlers
evaluationCriteria.description: Each event type triggers a different handler with appropriate behavior

evaluationCriteria.label: Asynchronous processing
evaluationCriteria.description: Events are processed asynchronously as they arrive, not batched

evaluationCriteria.label: Cross-event context
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent maintains context across related events like email threads
